title: dianthus × paradoxus
intro: Dianthus × paradoxus, also known as the fragrant pink or fringed pink, is a hybrid plant species in the carnation family, characterized by its delicate pink flowers with fringed petals. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Dianthus × paradoxus include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and act as a natural expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Dianthus × paradoxus can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for various health concerns.
